description: Companies providing intangible value through professional expertise, consulting, outsourcing, or managed services. Includes IT services, management consulting, accounting/legal firms, facility management, staffing agencies, and business process outsourcing. Mid-market service firms typically have 50-500 billable professionals, utilization rates of 65-85%, and revenues of €10-100M. Revenue is primarily project-based or recurring (managed services contracts).

processes.name: Lead-to-Contract (L2C)
processes.description: Business development process from initial lead identification through qualification, proposal writing, commercial negotiation, contract drafting, legal review, and contract signing. Includes statement of work (SOW) creation and pricing approval workflows.

processes.name: Project Delivery (Plan-to-Deliver)
processes.description: Project lifecycle management from project initiation, resource assignment, work breakdown structure, milestone tracking, deliverable review and approval, change request management, and project closure. Uses PRINCE2, PMI, or Agile frameworks.

processes.name: Time and Expense Management
processes.description: Consultant time recording against projects/clients, expense claim submission and approval, integration with payroll and project cost accounting. Critical for accurate client billing and project profitability analysis.

processes.name: Invoice-to-Cash (I2C)
processes.description: Client invoicing based on time & materials records, fixed-fee milestones, or recurring subscription, including invoice approval workflow, dispatch (electronic/paper), accounts receivable tracking, cash application, and collections.

processes.name: Resource Planning and Utilization
processes.description: Workforce capacity planning, skills-based resource allocation to projects, utilization rate monitoring, bench management (unbilled staff), and recruitment pipeline management to meet project demand.

processes.name: Knowledge Management
processes.description: Capture and reuse of project deliverables, methodologies, templates, client presentations, and lessons learned. Includes document version control, taxonomy management, and search indexing. Critical for delivery quality and efficiency.

processes.name: Subcontractor and Partner Management
processes.description: Onboarding of third-party consultants and service partners, contract management, rate card negotiation, purchase order processing, time sheet validation, and vendor invoice approval.

processes.name: Client Relationship Management (CRM to NPS)
processes.description: Ongoing client communication, satisfaction measurement (NPS surveys), account review meetings, contract renewal management, and upsell/cross-sell opportunity identification. Includes client-specific SLA monitoring.

kpis.name: Billable Utilization Rate
kpis.unit: % of available consultant hours billed to clients

kpis.name: Invoice-to-Payment Cycle (DSO)
kpis.unit: days from invoice date to cash receipt

kpis.name: Time Sheet Submission Rate (On-Time)
kpis.unit: % of time sheets submitted by deadline

kpis.name: Proposal Win Rate
kpis.unit: % of submitted proposals resulting in contract

kpis.name: Invoice Dispute Rate
kpis.unit: % of issued invoices disputed by clients

kpis.name: Knowledge Reuse Rate
kpis.unit: % of deliverable components reused from previous projects

kpis.name: New Employee Ramp-Up Time
kpis.unit: months to reach full billable utilization

pain_points: Manual time sheet processing and approval causes delayed invoicing (7-14 days post-period end), directly impacting cash flow and DSO performance
Inconsistent contract and SOW document management across sales, legal, and delivery teams leads to scope disputes and revenue leakage of 2-5%
Paper-based or email-driven expense claim workflows create month-end bottlenecks, with claims arriving weeks after the expense was incurred
Lack of centralized knowledge repository forces consultants to recreate deliverables from scratch, wasting 15-20% of billable time on non-value-added work
Disconnected CRM, project management, and billing systems require triple data entry and cause inconsistencies in client records
Manual invoice generation from Excel or isolated billing tools creates errors, especially for complex multi-milestone fixed-fee projects
Poor visibility into consultant availability and skills prevents optimal resource allocation, causing project delays and under-utilization of senior talent
Compliance documentation for ISO 27001, GDPR DPIA, and client security assessments is scattered across email threads, creating audit risk
Subcontractor invoice processing lacks automated PO/timesheet matching, leading to overpayment and budget overruns of 5-10%
Electronic signature adoption is low; wet signatures for contracts and NDAs delay project kickoff by 3-10 business days
typical_systems: PSA (Professional Services Automation): Salesforce PSA, FinancialForce, NetSuite OpenAir, Kantata (Mavenlink), Replicon
ERP/Finance: SAP Business One, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, Sage Intacct, NetSuite ERP
CRM: Salesforce Sales Cloud, Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, HubSpot, Pipedrive
Project Management: Microsoft Project, Jira/Confluence, Asana, Monday.com, Smartsheet
Document Management: SharePoint Online, Google Workspace, Notion, Confluence, M-Files
E-signature: DocuSign, Adobe Acrobat Sign, HelloSign (Dropbox Sign), SignNow
HR/Payroll: Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, BambooHR, Personio (EU), HiBob
Expense Management: Concur (SAP), Expensify, Zoho Expense, Rydoo (EU-focused)
regulatory_considerations: GDPR – Client personal data in project deliverables, CRM records, and HR systems; DPA agreements required with clients when processing their customers' data
ISO 27001 – Information security management increasingly required by enterprise clients as vendor qualification criterion
Professional licensing – Audit firms (statutory audit license), legal firms (bar association), medical consulting (healthcare licenses)
Employment law – Posted workers directive (cross-border project deployment), working time regulations, contractor vs. employee classification
Anti-money laundering (AML) – Accounting and legal firms subject to AML/KYC requirements for client onboarding
E-invoicing – Growing mandate for B2G and B2B invoicing in EU; affects service firms billing public sector and large corporate clients
Data residency – Multi-national clients may require data processing within specific jurisdictions (EU data sovereignty requirements)
ESG reporting – Growing demand for Scope 3 emissions documentation from clients requiring supplier ESG data for their own reporting (CSRD)
